leadership.bio: With over 20 years of experience in motor control and power electronics, Dr. Sammoud founded Appcon Technologies in 2004 with a clear mission: to make sensorless motor control viable for mass-production applications. His journey began as a research assistant, continued in senior engineering roles at a leading German corporation, and culminated in building Appcon into a trusted R&D partner for companies worldwide.

dmk.name: DMK
dmk.hero: One instrument. Replaces your entire motor test bench.
description: The DMK is a compact and powerful measurement solution designed for inverter-driven motors. It precisely measures phase currents and PWM voltages and synchronizes them with the rotor position, enabling real-time visualization and high-resolution data storage.

The motor voltages are measured using a unique demodulation technique that reconstructs the effective PWM voltages directly from the inverter signals without the need for analog filtering. This approach preserves the true time relationship between currents, voltages, and rotor position, allowing highly accurate analysis of the drive system.

Beyond electrical measurements, the DMK also computes the inverter output and motor state variables in the dq coordinate system. This makes it possible to directly observe the key FOC control variables during operation, providing deep insight into the dynamic behavior of the motor drive.

Thanks to its high time resolution and synchronized measurements, the DMK allows engineers to detect inverter and control issues even within sub-PWM time intervals, making it an exceptionally powerful tool for development, benchmarking, and endurance testing.

By combining current measurement, PWM voltage reconstruction, rotor position synchronization, and advanced drive analysis in a single device, the DMK effectively replaces oscilloscopes, current probes, data loggers, and PWM monitoring equipment — reducing cost, complexity, and engineering effort.

highlights.label: Real Time PWM Decoder
highlights.detail: Reconstructs effective PWM voltages in real time without analog filtering

featureBlocks.title: Power electronic testing
featureBlocks.body: The unique PWM decoding for high-frequency, high-resolution signals beyond typical instrument capabilities. Its advanced voltage measurement decodes PWM in real time, revealing the exact inverter voltages as the motor experiences them. Without common filtering, any inverter malfunction is easily detected rather than masked.

featureBlocks.title: Development engineers in motor control
featureBlocks.body: As a compact, multifunctional measurement instrument that replaces multiple separate devices. They connect DMK to their motor/inverter system to gather analog signals and key motor-control variables for scope and visualize in real time with the DMK PC-Software. Access internal state variables of both the motor and the inverter directly. Record and store data for extended analysis.

featureBlocks.title: Long-time tests
featureBlocks.body: Measure and log all motor and inverter state variables over an extended period. Identify anomalies or performance degradation that might occur only after hours, days, or weeks of continuous operation. Automated data analysis on the extensive datasets provided by the DMK helps uncover hidden or statistically rare problems that are difficult for humans to detect.

featureBlocks.title: Benchmarking and reverse engineering
featureBlocks.body: Analyze third-party or competitor drive systems with minimal setup, tapping directly into motor and inverter signals. DMK gathers precise data for fair performance comparisons—efficiency, accuracy, control strategy—and provides actionable insights to optimize designs, inform purchases, and guide partnerships.

dmk.demoEmbedTitle: One instrument at the centre of your test bench
dmk.demoEmbedIntro: The DMK connects directly to your PC for live visualization, to an oscilloscope via analog outputs, and to USB storage for long-term recording. Real-time measurement and analysis from a single compact unit.

demoEmbedList.desc: Configure the device, view live waveforms, and run analysis from your laptop or desktop.
Use the analog outputs to monitor signals on any scope; no need for a separate PC during testing.
Record hours of data to an external SSD (up to 2TB) for later offline analysis.
Remote access and streaming for integration into automated test rigs.

dmk.photoAlt: DMK-RT connected between inverter and motor
dmk.pcSceneTabsAria: DMK software tabs, click to switch view
dmk.hmiDataLoggerAlt: DMK Database Viewer with Plot — load data by time range, plot and export
dmk.hmiDataMonitorAlt: DMK Fault Detector and Plotter — detect and visualize faults in voltage data

dataIntel.ariaLabel: DMK Data Intelligence — recording, anomaly detection, and monitoring
dataIntel.title: Record, analyze, and monitor — like never before
dataIntel.lead: Record waveforms like video, run custom or built-in algorithms to detect anomalies and spikes, and monitor in real time. Optional AI for behavior detection.
dataIntel.recordBody: Capture waveforms over time and replay them. Long-term recording to storage with full time resolution.
dataIntel.detectBody: Built-in and custom algorithms to detect anomalies, spikes, or any pattern you define in real time.
dataIntel.algoBody: Run your own detection logic on live or recorded data. Perfect for fleet or test-rig automation.
dataIntel.monitorBody: Monitor one or many units (e.g. vehicles or test rigs) with algorithms checking for whatever you need.
dataIntel.cta: From development to fleet: one tool for visualization, logging, and intelligent analysis.
dataIntel.storyLiveBody: The DMK gives you a real-time window into your motor's electrical behaviour — 3-phase voltages, currents, FFT analysis — rendered as an oscilloscope, live, at up to 250 kHz sampling rate. Every sine wave, every transient, every ripple. You can see it all.
dataIntel.storyProblemTitle: Traditional motor monitoring is blind to reality.
dataIntel.storyProblemBody: Most motor monitoring systems give you after-the-fact data — averages, trends, and alerts that arrive too late. You miss the critical electrical events that precede failure.
dataIntel.storyPain1Body: By the time you notice a problem, damage has already begun
dataIntel.storyPain2Body: Missing real-time electrical signatures and transient events
dataIntel.storyPain3Body: Too much noise, not enough actionable insights
dataIntel.storySolBody: The DMK transforms complex motor data into instant, actionable insights. See what's happening inside your motor — as it happens.
dataIntel.storyStep1Body: Capture electrical signals at 250 kHz sampling rate with precision measurement hardware. Every voltage fluctuation, current spike, and harmonic distortion is recorded in real-time.
dataIntel.storyStep2Body: Continuous, timestamped acquisition across all channels. The DMK writes every sample directly to a structured local database — 8 channels simultaneously, at up to 250 kHz — with no gaps, no manual triggers, and no data loss. Every session is queryable, replayable, and exportable the moment recording stops.
dataIntel.storyStep2Detail1: 8-channel simultaneous logging — U_V, V_W, W_U, Is1–3, Angle, Udc
dataIntel.storyStep2Detail2: Microsecond-precision timestamps across the full session
dataIntel.storyStep2Detail3: Navigate any time window — load, zoom, and replay any moment
dataIntel.storyStep3Body: Advanced algorithms analyze electrical signatures to predict failures before they occur. Machine learning models identify subtle patterns that indicate developing issues.
dataIntel.storyStep3Detail3: Continuous learning — improves with motor data
dataIntel.storyStep4Body: Intelligent fault detection — your rules or ours. Point the Data Monitor at any logged session and run a detection pass. Choose from our built-in algorithms — Rate of Change, Absolute Threshold — or define your own logic entirely. The system scans the full dataset, indexes every event, and plots each fault with a ±10 ms context window across all three phases.
dataIntel.storyStep4Detail1: Pre-built algorithms — Rate of Change and Absolute Threshold, ready to deploy
dataIntel.storyStep4Detail2: Custom algorithm support — define your own detection conditions
dataIntel.storyStep4Detail3: Fault library — every event indexed, selectable, and export-ready in one click
dataIntel.addYourAlgorithmOrOurs: Add your own algorithm or ask us to add one.
dataIntel.faultStatusFound: Fault 2 detected in 1.8 ms — V_W anomaly at T = 0
dataIntel.sectionLead: Load, plot, detect faults, and run AI-driven analysis on your motor data.
dataIntel.highSpeedAcquisitionTitle: DMKRT Motor Monitoring System — time and DQ chart view
dataIntel.databaseViewerCaption: Select a database, set time range, load data, and plot variables. Export to CSV or image.
dataIntel.faultDetectorCaption: Configure detection (e.g. rate of change), detect faults, and visualize voltage data around each event.
dataIntel.aiDrivenCaption: Pattern recognition and behavior analysis on live or recorded data for predictive insights.

lci.name: LCI
lci.hero:
Low-Cost Inverter Platform for
Rapid Motor Drive Development
lci.problemImageAlt: Mechanical and electrical domains — the challenge of integrating both.
lci.shiftImageAlt: LCI as a modular bridge connecting your product to a working motor.
lci.problemHeadline: Your team builds great products. But the electronics keep slowing you down.
problemBullets: Your engineers are mechanical experts — not power electronics specialists.
Every new motor integration means months of custom inverter design.
You're solving a problem someone already solved.
lci.shiftHeadline: With LCI you save months of inverter hardware development
shiftBodyParagraphs: When developing a new inverter platform, engineering teams often invest significant effort in solving fundamental hardware challenges before they can focus on the actual product innovation. Selecting power components, designing reliable gate driving, integrating current sensing, and ensuring stable and efficient operation can consume a large portion of the development time.

With the LCI platform, there is no need to reinvent the wheel. The inverter hardware has already been carefully designed, tested, and optimized to achieve a minimal component count and low hardware cost. Instead of starting from scratch, development teams can use LCI as a proven motor control hardware platform and concentrate their engineering effort on the unique features and added value of their product.

While many semiconductor vendors provide open-source or reference inverter designs to promote their hardware components, the LCI approach goes one step further. Appcon supports partners in integrating the platform into their own hardware architecture, accompanying them throughout the development process. Our focus is not to promote a particular hardware brand, but to ensure that the final system architecture serves the technical and economic goals of the product and the customer.
lci.whyHeadline: Built for scale. Proven in production.
lci.ctaBody: Talk to our team about integrating LCI into your next product.

amc.name: AMC
amc.hero:
Universal Sensorless PM-Motor Control Driver.
Any Motor. Any Inverter.
description: Appcon Motor Control (AMC) is a motor control driver software library designed for controlling permanent magnet motors in a wide range of drive applications. The library can be integrated directly into a customer's firmware, enabling reliable motor control with or without a position sensor.

AMC is a software-only solution that eliminates the need for a mechanical position sensor by accurately estimating motor speed and rotor position while maintaining performance comparable to sensored Field-Oriented Control (FOC).

The technology behind AMC is the result of more than 20 years of industrial experience in sensorless control of PMSM drives, with implementations running in products manufactured in millions of units every year.

Designed for practical use in real products, AMC combines high performance, robustness, and ease of integration. Features such as automatic motor identification allow developers to configure the control quickly, even without deep expertise in motor control.
As a plug-and-play control module, AMC can be seamlessly integrated into existing drive software architectures, enabling rapid deployment of advanced motor control capabilities.

benefits.title: Easy integration in existing drive software
benefits.body: Clear interface definitions inspired by the AUTOSAR standard.

benefits.title: Optimization and version upgrades independent of the application
benefits.body: Validation via Software-in-the-Loop and with advanced evaluation boards.

benefits.title: One control software for all motors and inverters
benefits.body: Easy hardware migration and configuration for new motors.

benefits.title: Maximum flexibility for the application software
benefits.body: Easy and secure development of drive applications using AMC as the motor driver.

amc.gameChangerHeadline: A game-changing solution for sensorless control of PMSM
motorControlItems: Maximum torque and dynamics from standstill to >100k RPM
Automatic motor identification and controller parameter configuration
Advanced features including battery current limitation
sensorlessItems: No motor parameters needed for position estimation: the motor acts as the sensor
Full torque at standstill and low speeds
Superior performance compared to position sensors at high speeds, with smooth and accurate speed and position information
amc.integrationAlt: AMC integration: Application and AMC interface with hardware — PCBs and motors
amc.sensorlessDiagramAlt: AMC sensorless motor control block diagram: microcontroller, inverter, PMSM

rows.left: Oscilloscope + current probes + data logger + PWM monitor + cabling

rows.right: No sensor; motor acts as sensor

expertise.label: Expertise

consulting.label: Technology Consulting
paragraphs: At Appcon Technologies, we have been working in sensorless motor control for more than 20 years, collaborating with international companies on drive systems used in millions of products every year. Our motor control firmware runs reliably in high-volume applications across industries such as white goods, fans, pumps and automotive-related systems.

This long-term involvement in real industrial projects has given us deep practical knowledge of complete drive systems from mechanical dynamics and motor behavior to power electronics and embedded real-time control software.

One of the most common challenges we see in engineering teams is the gap between a working prototype and a robust serial product. Many motor control solutions perform well in the lab but encounter difficulties when they must meet the requirements of cost, reliability, manufacturability, and scalability in mass production.

Our experience across all disciplines of drive control and mechatronics, combined with a strong track record of bringing projects successfully into serial production, allows us to support our partners precisely at this critical stage.

At Appcon Technologies, we create advanced motor control solutions engineered for serial production environments. Our technologies combine deep control expertise with practical implementation knowledge, enabling our partners to deploy high-performance and cost-efficient drive systems in demanding industrial products.

By combining deep technical expertise with practical industrial experience, Appcon enables its partners to stay at the forefront of motor control technology and achieve measurable advantages over their competitors.

hardware.label: Hardware Engineering
paragraphs: Our hardware development team specializes in the meticulous design and development of motor control electronics, specifically inverters, to cater to the unique needs of our clients. Our process begins with clients providing their specific requirements, initiating a collaborative journey where we draw upon our extensive experience to design robust power electronics.

With a wealth of expertise in handling complex power electronic designs, our team navigates the intricate details of layout and design with precision. We are well-versed in optimizing boards for both performance and cost-effectiveness, ensuring the delivery of efficient solutions.
list: Product redesign for cost reduction & efficiency
hardware.conceptionLead: From circuit schematics to production-ready PCB layout — we use industry-standard EDA tools to deliver robust, optimized designs.

software.label: Software Development
paragraphs: Our team develops embedded software according to the specifications and the functionality needs provided by the customer. We deliver robust, real-time control solutions across multiple domains.
list: Real-Time Field Oriented Control Embedded Software (FOC, HF-Sensorless, BEMF-Sensorless)

mbd.label: Model-Based Design
paragraphs: Automation of Model-Based Design processes like automatic code generation, model reusability, continuous testing, and documentation results in faster time to market and lower product cost, something that companies are always looking for.
Our teams use Model-Based Design to provide you with a motor control solution that is tailored to your specific requirements. They can:
list: Provide the expertise to help you migrate to Model-Based Design
Design from scratch and validate your motor control system in a simulation environment, giving your design team the ability to perform rapid prototyping
Provide expertise to evaluate, modify, and maintain your existing motor control models

Create an interface and integrate the automatically generated code from the controller model into your existing software or provide you a full range motor control software solution using Model-Based Design
mbd.typhoonTitle: Typhoon HIL as our real-time emulation
mbd.typhoonText: For hardware-in-the-loop validation of our control models, we rely on Typhoon HIL platforms. This allows us to run real-time emulation of power stages and controllers, close the loop with physical hardware, and deliver Model-Based Design projects that are already proven on HIL before they reach your lab.

benefits.title: Cost-Effective
benefits.desc: Reduces development time and effort, leading to lower costs and faster time-to-market.

benefits.title: Improved Accuracy
benefits.desc: Early simulation and testing reduce design errors and enhance overall reliability.

benefits.title: Self-Documenting
benefits.desc: Easy to share and maintain across teams.

benefits.title: Appcon-Library
benefits.desc: Enables rapid development and automatic generation of efficient code.

prototyping.label: Prototyping
prototyping.heroLead: High-quality product prototyping — multi-layer PCBs, SMD and THT assembly, from concept to physical reality.
paragraphs: We provide an end-to-end service covering PCB, stencil and component sourcing, board assembly, and functional testing, all from a single source.

about.pageDescription: Founded in 2004, Appcon Technologies is an R&D engineering firm specializing in sensorless control of electrical motors and power electronics.
about.paragraph1: Founded in 2004 by Dr.-Ing. Hafedh Sammoud, Appcon Technologies is an R&D engineering firm specializing in sensorless control of electrical motors and the conceptualization and optimization of power electronics.
about.paragraph2: Our mission is to advance the state-of-the-art in sensorless motor control solutions, enabling their application in new scenarios where it is currently unfeasible. Particularly in high-volume products, where energy-efficient drives are underutilized due to cost constraints, we believe that our efforts contribute to optimizing energy consumption and reducing global CO₂ emissions.
about.highlight1Body: Highly qualified engineers with diverse expertise in drive technology, simulation, programming, and practical electronics.
about.highlight2Body: International focus — we communicate with our partners in German, English, and French.
about.highlight3Body:
Collaborating with companies in
white goods, fans, pumps, power tools,
and automotive applications,
with solutions used in systems produced
in millions of units annually.

mission.pageDescription: Driving innovation in sensorless motor control solutions to enable energy-efficient drives across industries worldwide.
mission.missionStatement: The mission of Appcon Technologies is to advance the state-of-the-art in sensorless motor control solutions.
mission.missionText: We dedicate ourselves to pushing the boundaries of what's possible in motor control and power electronics, developing solutions that reduce cost, eliminate complexity, and deliver higher reliability through sensorless technology.
mission.philosophySlogan:
More than just 'know how'
we have the 'know why'
mission.philosophyText1: At Appcon, we don't just implement solutions — we understand the fundamental principles that make them work. This deep understanding allows us to innovate, optimize, and solve problems that others cannot. When you work with us, you gain access to decades of research-backed expertise in motor control theory and practice.
mission.philosophyText2: Our engineers bring academic rigor combined with practical industry experience, enabling us to translate complex theoretical concepts into robust, production-ready systems.
mission.value1Body: Continuously pushing the boundaries of sensorless control technology
mission.value2Body: Delivering solutions that exceed expectations in quality and reliability
mission.value3Body: Collaborating closely with clients from concept through production
mission.value4Body: Enabling energy-efficient drives that reduce global CO₂ emissions
mission.visionText1: We envision a future where energy-efficient motor drives are ubiquitous — from household appliances and power tools to automotive and e-mobility applications. Through our work in sensorless control, we aim to eliminate the need for expensive encoders and sensors, reducing cost and complexity while improving reliability.
mission.visionText2: With systems in production numbering in the millions annually, we are proud to contribute to global energy optimization and a more sustainable future. Our vision is to remain at the forefront of motor control innovation, continuously advancing the technology that powers the world.

ourStory.pageDescription: Two decades of turning academic research into practical solutions for sensorless motor control and power electronics.
ourStory.block1Lead: Appcon Technologies was founded by Dr.-Ing. Hafedh Sammoud with a clear mission: to bridge the gap between advanced research in sensorless motor control and the needs of industry. What began as a small R&D team has grown into a trusted partner for companies worldwide in white goods, power tools, automotive, and e-mobility.
ourStory.block1Text: From the start, we focused on turning theoretical advances into reliable, cost-effective solutions. Our work in field-oriented control (FOC), high-frequency injection, and sensorless algorithms has enabled energy-efficient drives in applications where precision and cost matter most.
ourStory.block2Lead: Over the years we have expanded our capabilities across technology consulting, hardware engineering, software development, model-based design, and prototyping. We collaborate with clients from concept through to production, delivering solutions that combine academic rigor with real-world applicability.
ourStory.block2Text: Our commitment to energy efficiency and innovation has remained at the core of everything we do. We continue to invest in R&D and to share our work at leading conferences and trade shows such as PCIM Europe, where we connect with experts and showcase new developments in motor control and power electronics.
ourStory.block3Lead: Today, Appcon Technologies remains dedicated to advancing the state-of-the-art in sensorless control. We enable energy-efficient drives in high-volume products where they were once underutilized due to cost constraints, and we believe our efforts contribute to optimizing energy consumption and reducing global CO₂ emissions.
ourStory.block3Text: We are proud to work with partners who produce systems in millions annually—from household appliances and power tools to automotive and e-mobility. Our story is one of continuous innovation, collaboration, and a steadfast focus on turning research into solutions that matter.

events.pageDescription: Where we connect with experts and showcase our latest innovations in motor control and power electronics.

events.innovation1Body: Sensorless control with full torque at standstill
events.photoCaption: Dr. Hafedh Sammoud at PCIM Europe 2025, Nuremberg

contact.pageDescription: Have questions about our motor control solutions or want to discuss your project? We're here to help.
contact.privacyNotePrefix: We use your details only to respond to your message. See our
contact.privacyNoteSuffix: for how we handle your data.
contact.intro: Reach out to us and our team will get back to you as soon as possible.
contact.submitSuccessDetail: We'll reply within a few business days.
contact.submitError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

privacyPage.pageDescription: How we collect, use, and protect your information when you use our website and contact form.
privacyPage.section1Body: Appcon Technologies ("we", "us", "our") operates this website. We are an R&D engineering firm specializing in sensorless motor control and power electronics. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle your personal data when you use our site or contact us.
privacyPage.section2IntroSuffix: , we collect only what you choose to send:
privacyPage.section2Note: We do not use a database to store form submissions. Your message is sent to us via a third‑party form service (e.g. Formspree) and/or by email. We do not collect personal data for any purpose other than responding to your inquiry.
privacyPage.section3Intro: We use the data you provide only to:
privacyPage.section3ListItem2: Manage our business relationship with you if you are or become a client or partner
privacyPage.section3Note: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing. We do not use your email or message content for any purpose other than as described above.
privacyPage.section4Body: Contact form submissions are processed by a form provider (e.g. Formspree) and delivered to our team by email. We may retain correspondence in our email systems for as long as needed to fulfill your request and for legitimate business and legal purposes. We do not store your data in a separate database on this website.
privacyPage.section5Body: We treat your data as confidential. Our website uses HTTPS so data in transit is encrypted. We take reasonable steps to keep the data we receive (e.g. via email) secure and to limit access to those who need it to respond to you. No system is 100% secure; we do not guarantee absolute security but we work to protect your information.
privacyPage.section6Intro: Depending on where you live, you may have the right to:
privacyPage.section6ListItem1: Ask what personal data we hold about you
privacyPage.section6ListItem2: Request correction or deletion of your data
privacyPage.section6ListItem3: Object to or restrict certain processing
privacyPage.section6ListItem4: Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
privacyPage.section6Note: To exercise these rights or ask questions about this policy, contact us using the contact form or the details on our Contact page.
privacyPage.section7Body: We may use essential cookies (e.g. for language preference) and, if applicable, analytics cookies to understand how visitors use our site. We will not use non‑essential cookies without your consent where required by law. You can change your cookie preferences in your browser settings.
privacyPage.section8Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The "Last updated" date at the top will be revised when we do. We encourage you to check this page occasionally for changes.
privacyPage.section9Body: For any questions about this Privacy Policy or our use of your data, please contact us via the contact form or the details provided on our Contact page.
